name: Marcus T.
text: honestly this is the cleanest write up of it I've seen. I joined ICA early 2024 - so just past 2 years now - and the framework still holds. one thing the article doesn't mention: their team is genuinely responsive when something weird happens with your accounts. I had a suspension scare last August and got real help within a few hours.

name: Devon K.
text: Was skeptical at first because every "AI passive income" pitch sounds the same. Joined the program in March 2024 to test. Two years later I'm still in it. Reason: when I had an inventory writedown question last month, I emailed them and got an actual structured answer back the same day. That's not normal for a program at this price.

name: Anita Rashid
text: The workshop is solid. I went in expecting fluff and got an actual framework. Joined the full program shortly after, around late spring 2024. Still in touch with the team whenever an account issue or strategy question comes up - that ongoing access is honestly the part I'd pay for separately if they unbundled it.

name: Jay
text: in for 2 years. yes the workshop is 90 min not 60 (article should fix). yes the stack works. yes they pick up when you have a problem. that's it that's the whole comment

name: Priya M.
text: Came across this article from a friend and wanted to add: the article is right that the program isn't a hype machine. I'm coming up on my 2-year anniversary in ICA this June. The thing that surprised me most was how often I still email the team with random product or account questions - and how reliably they respond. That kind of post-purchase support is rare in this space.

name: Connor B.
text: Good summary. The workshop convinced me to enrol back in 2024 and I've stuck with it. One thing that's worth flagging for newcomers: don't expect a get-rich-quick result. It's a real workflow that takes 60-90 days to start producing. The team will tell you that directly if you ask them - which I appreciate because most programs hide that detail.

name: Lina O.
text: the part about staying in touch with the team for account issues is what kept me in the program for two years. amazon will throw curveballs - suspensions, listing flags, weird ASIN delistings - and having someone who knows the platform on the other end of an email is the difference between a panic week and a normal week. worth more than the curriculum honestly.
